title: Madgaon–Hapa Superfast Express
summary: The 22907 / 22908 Madgaon–Hapa Superfast Express is a Superfast train belonging to Western Railway zone that runs between Madgaon Junction and Hapa in India. It is currently being operated with 22907/22908 train numbers on a weekly basis.
text:
The 22907 / 22908 Madgaon–Hapa Superfast Express is a Superfast train belonging to Western Railway zone that runs between Madgaon Junction and Hapa in India. It is currently being operated with 22907/22908 train numbers on a weekly basis.

Coach composition
The train has standard ICF rakes with a maximum speed of 110 km/h. The train consists of 23 coaches:

1 AC II Tier
5 AC III Tier
10 Sleeper
1 Pantry Car
4 General Unreserved
2 Seating cum Luggage Rake

Service
The 22907/Madgaon–Hapa Superfast Express has an average speed of 61 km/h and covers 1348 km in 22 hrs 15 mins.
The 22908/Hapa–Madgaon Superfast Express has an average speed of 58 km/h and covers 1348 km in 23 hrs 20 mins.

Schedule
Route and halts
The important halts of the train are:

Madgaon Junction
Thivim
Kudal
Ratnagiri
Khed
Panvel
Vasai Road
Vapi
Surat
Bharuch Junction
Vadodara Junction
Anand Junction
Nadiad Junction
Ahmedabad Junction
Viramgam Junction
Surendranagar Junction
Rajkot Junction
Hapa

Traction
Both trains are hauled by a Vatva Loco Shed based WAP-4 or Vadodara Loco Shed based WAP-5 electric locomotive from Madgoan to Hapa and vice versa

Rake sharing
The train shares its rake with 19577/19578 Jamnagar Tirunelveli Express.
